page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 80 dedicated virtual CPUs and 160.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, aligning the instance with compute-heavy resource profiles.

The instance does not include integrated local storage or bundled GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the configuration provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Due to its high vCPU density relative to memory, the n4d-highcpu-80 offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require substantial parallel processing power without massive memory footprints. This resource balance makes it suitable for compute-intensive tasks such as batch processing, high-performance web serving, distributed analytics, and software compilation pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-80 is an x86_64 architecture server within the n4d family, configured with 80 dedicated vCPUs and 160.0 GB of RAM. This allocation provides a compute-optimized ratio of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance features no local storage and no integrated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for high-density compute workloads, this server offers a cost-effective profile for parallel processing tasks that do not demand large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include batch processing, software compilation, distributed analytics, and high-throughput web serving.
